title: Pentagon announces withdrawal of 5,000 US troops from Germany within 6-12 months
summary: The Pentagon confirmed withdrawal of approximately 5,000 US troops from Germany, representing 14% of the 36,000 American service members stationed there, following Trump's clash with German Chancellor Merz over the Iran war.
contents:
## US Withdraws 5,000 Troops From Germany

### Pentagon Announcement
The United States confirmed on May 2, 2026, that approximately 5,000 troops would withdraw from Germany within the next six to twelve months. Pentagon spokesman Sean Parnell stated the decision followed "a thorough review of the Department's force posture in Europe" and recognized "theater requirements and conditions on the ground."

### Scale and Impact
The withdrawal represents 14 percent of the 36,000 American service members currently stationed in Germany. Germany hosts several critical US military facilities, including the headquarters of European Command and Africa Command, Ramstein Air Base, and the Landstuhl medical center. US nuclear missiles also remain stationed in the country.

### Political Context
President Trump threatened the withdrawal after German Chancellor Friedrich Merz criticized US strategy in the Iran war, saying the US appeared "humiliated" by Iranian leadership. Trump responded by telling Merz to "spend more time on ending the war with Russia/Ukraine" and "fixing his broken Country."

### Congressional and Expert Reaction
Senator Jack Reed, ranking Democrat on the Senate Armed Services Committee, condemned the move, stating it "suggests American commitments to our allies depend on the president's mood." Bradley Bowman of the Foundation for Defense of Democracies warned that the US military presence in Germany "strengthens deterrence against additional Kremlin aggression" and facilitates power projection into the Mediterranean, Middle East, and Africa.
